title: DCM Data Layer record
description: Machine-validatable Data Layer (foundations/layering-and-versioning.md; DCM ADR-012). A request payload is assembled by merging ordered layers lowest-authority-first; every field a layer sets records that layer's uuid as its provenance source. UDLM carries the layer records; DCM's assembly engine merges them. Layers carry DATA (the what); Policies (policy.schema.json) decide/transform (the how). data-model-core.md §2 [D8.4].

uuid.description: RFC 9562 v4 identity. This uuid is the field-provenance source recorded for every field this layer sets (layering-and-versioning §provenance).

supersedes.description: LINEAGE — the single, explicit mechanism (UDLM ADR-012; the decision-record 'never edit, supersede' idiom). A layer version is IMMUTABLE: any change mints a NEW record (new uuid + new version), never editing the old one, and this array names the uuid(s) of the version(s) it directly supersedes. Absent = a lineage ROOT (a first version, or a genuinely new entity). Because it is a uuid array it expresses ANY lineage shape — linear (supersedes the one prior), fork (two records each supersede the same prior), merge (one record supersedes several), rename (supersede a prior at a different handle). It is authoritative (uuid-based); `handle` is advisory display only and is NOT a lineage signal. Each uuid MUST resolve to an existing layer of the same reference_data_type with a strictly lower version (validate.py check_layer_lineage). Change-impact is derived from this DAG plus the reverse reference index — which records reference a version that has since been superseded.

tenant_uuid.description: Owning tenant (TEN-001). system/platform-domain layers (base, core, reference_data) are owned by the platform tenant; tenant-domain layers by the consuming tenant.

layer_type.description: The six+ layer types (layering-and-versioning §3): base (foundation, every chain begins here), core (standards/compliance defaults), intermediate (org/customization), service (catalog/provider), request (consumer's direct declarations — highest data authority), policy (applied over the merged result), reference_data (shared lookup data).

reference_data_type.description: REQUIRED when layer_type is reference_data (enforced below): the kind of shared dataset this layer governs — e.g. location, os_image, vm_size, network_zone, storage_class, gpu_profile, environment, topology_capability (layering-and-versioning §3.7). A data reference ({ref_uuid, ref_name, reference_data_type}; UDLM ADR-012) is only valid against a reference_data layer of the SAME reference_data_type — the type is the match axis the reference declares. Meaningless on non-reference_data layers.

precedence_class.description: Position in the assembly precedence chain, lowest-authority first: base → core → intermediate → service → request, with policy applied over the result. Higher classes override lower field-by-field (narrow-never-widen for constraint profiles, E1). Usually equals layer_type; reference_data layers are looked up, not merged, so they declare their functional class.

precedence_order.description: Optional fine-grained ordering WITHIN a precedence_class (e.g. multiple stacked intermediate layers); higher wins. Ties break by class then by declared chain order.

domain.description: Ownership/precedence scope. resource_type-scoped layers MUST declare resource_type.

resource_type.description: When the layer is type-scoped: the Category.Type it applies to. A type-scoped base layer must conform to that type's universal field requirements (layering-and-versioning §3.1).

contributor.description: Who may author this layer + the review it requires (layering-and-versioning §2; federated-contribution-model §3).

review.description: request layers = none (direct consumer declaration); all others flow through the GitOps PR review model.

fields.description: The DATA this layer contributes to the assembled payload — dot-path-or-nested field values. Each field set here records THIS layer's uuid as its provenance source. Values are typed by the target Resource Type Spec and are not re-validated here (the layer is a partial contribution, not a complete entity).

narrow_only.description: When true, this is a constraint/narrowing layer (E1): it may only TIGHTEN field markers/values declared by lower layers, never loosen them (narrow-never-widen). Recorded so the assembly engine enforces the direction.
